content.full_text:
D-76-12-032

December 22, 1976


Clifford Heisterberg
Heisterberg and Doubet
357 Bancroft Avenue
San Leandro, California 94577

Dear Mr. Heisterberg:

Thank you for your recent letter in which you asked
whether the Political Reform Act prohibits musicians from
contributing their services to a political campaign. You
also inquired as to the reporting requirements, if any.

The Political Reform Act only prohibits contributions
by lobbyists. (Government Code Section 86202.) Assuming
none of the musicians are registered lobbyists, there is no
prohibition against their making any contribution.

The Act excludes from the definition of "contribution"
volunteer personal service (Government Code Section 82015,
paragraph 5). Thus, the volunteer services of a musician
are not reportable under the Act. However, other costs
related to the concert, e.g. the hall, ushers, advertising,
etc., would be a contribution if not paid for by the can-
didate. Further, if some of the musicians are being paid by
a third party (for example, the leader of the group pays a
drummer), then such expenditures must be reported.

The preceding discussion is rather general since your
inquiry asked broad questions. Should you have further
questions, please call me at (916) 322-6441.

Sincerely,


Robert M. Stern
General Counsel

RMS:ma

1-29

HEISTERBERG & DOUBET
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
367 BANCROFT AVENUE
SAN LEANDRO, CALIFORNIA 94577
TELEPHONE (415) 638-0300

December 3, 1976

Robert M. Stern, Chief Counsel
Fair Political Practice Commission
926 J Street - Suite 522
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Stern:

I have a question concerning contributions to political
campaigns by musical artists who stage performances and
share the proceeds with a political campaign. Would such
a transaction be prohibited by the Political Reform Act
or would it be considered voluntary services rendered
and, if so, how would it have to be reported?

Thank you for any assistance you might be able to provide.

Very truly yours,

HEISTERBERG & DOUBET

By Clifford E. Heisterberg

CEH:cm
